zh.lifeSummary: 當Catherine De Burgh於大約1397年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 吉爾達康瑙特 布里奇時，她的父親Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricarde34歲，母親Benomom O'Kelly19歲。她於1417年與Conor Na-Suidane O'Brien King of Thomond在愛爾蘭結婚。她去世於1480年在愛爾蘭庫迪 克萊爾恩尼斯享年84歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Catherine De Burgh于大约1397年出生在爱尔兰庫迪 吉爾達康瑙特 布里奇时，她的父亲Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricarde34岁，母亲Benomom O'Kelly19岁。她于1417年与Conor Na-Suidane O'Brien King of Thomond在爱尔兰结婚。她去世于1480年在爱尔兰庫迪 克萊爾恩尼斯享年84岁。

nameMeanings.name: Burgh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).
German: variant of   Burk  .

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.
South German: variant of   Buri  .

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: English:

topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Catherine De Burgh was born about 1397, in Connaught Bridge, County Kildare, Ireland, her father, Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricarde, was 34 and her mother, Benomom O'Kelly, was 19. She married Conor Na-Suidane O'Brien King of Thomond in 1417, in Ireland. She died in 1480, in Ennis, County Clare, Ireland, at the age of 84.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Catherine De Burgh est née environ 1397, à Connaught Bridge, County Kildare, Irlande, son père, Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricarde, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Benomom O'Kelly, avait 19 ans. Elle a épousé Conor Na-Suidane O'Brien King of Thomond en 1417, à Irlande. Elle est décédée en 1480, à Ennis, County Clare, Irlande, à l’âge de 84 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Catherine De Burgh um 1397 in Connaught Bridge, County Kildare, Irland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricarde 34 und ihre Mutter Benomom O'Kelly 19. Sie hat 1417 in Irland Conor Na-Suidane O'Brien King of Thomond geheiratet. Sie starb 1480 im Alter von 84 Jahren in Ennis, County Clare, Irland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Catherine De Burgh è nata il circa 1397 nel Connaught Bridge, County Kildare, Irlanda, sua padre, Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricarde, aveva 34 anni e sua madre, Benomom O'Kelly, aveva 19 anni. Lei sposato Conor Na-Suidane O'Brien King of Thomond nel 1417, nel Irlanda. Morì nel 1480, nel Ennis, County Clare, Irlanda, all'età di 84 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Catherine De Burghが約1397年にアイルランドコウントイ キルダアコナウト ブリッジで生まれた時，父Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricardeは34歳，母Benomom O'Kellyは19歳であった。1417年にアイルランドでConor Na-Suidane O'Brien King of Thomondと結婚した。彼女は1480年にアイルランドコウントイ クラーエニスで84歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Catherine De Burgh(이)가 대략 1397년 아일랜드 카운티 킬더르 콘오그h트 브리지에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricarde(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Benomom O'Kelly(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 1417년 아일랜드에서 Conor Na-Suidane O'Brien King of Thomond(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1480년 아일랜드 카운티 클래르 니스에서 84세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Catherine De Burgh nasceu aproximadamente 1397, em Connaught Bridge, County Kildare, Irlanda, seu pai, Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricarde, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Benomom O'Kelly, tinha 19 anos. Ela casou-se com Conor Na-Suidane O'Brien King of Thomond em 1417, em Irlanda. Ela faleceu em 1480, em Ennis, County Clare, Irlanda, com 84 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Catherine De Burgh родилась около 1397 г. в местности коннот бридж, коунти каилдар, Ирландия, её отцу по имени Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricarde было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Benomom O'Kelly, 19. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Conor Na-Suidane O'Brien King of Thomond в 1417 г. в местности Ирландия. Она умерла в 1480 г. в местности эннис, коунти клар, Ирландия в возрасте 84 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Catherine De Burgh nació aproximadamente 1397, en Connaught Bridge, County Kildare, Irlanda, su papá, Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricarde, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Benomom O'Kelly, tenía 19 años. Se casó con Conor Na-Suidane O'Brien King of Thomond en 1417, en Irlanda. Murió en 1480, en Ennis, County Clare, Irlanda, a la edad de 84 años.
